name: ERNIE 3.0 Titan
note: 260B knowledge-enhanced dense model (with Peng Cheng Lab); then the largest Chinese dense LM and first knowledge-enhanced 100B+ model.
verificationNotes:
Existence and 260B parameter count confirmed directly from the arXiv abstract (2112.12731: 'ERNIE 3.0 Titan ... up to 260 billion parameters'). Submission history on the arXiv page confirms v1 was submitted 23 Dec 2021, exactly matching the given releaseDate. Corroborated by MarkTechPost (Dec 29, 2021) coverage. No public release / API ever existed (paper-only model), consistent with closed/proprietary/apiAvailable:false. No benchmark results were carried since none were in the original record; ctxK (512) is a plausible but not independently re-confirmed figure for this specific checkpoint (the cited HF transformers doc covers a different, later open ERNIE model family, not Titan) — flagged as lower-confidence but left unchanged since it is a minor spec value on a paper-only, non-served model. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): FINAL ATTEMPT — no taxonomy-fitting public benchmark data exists for ERNIE 3.0 Titan (Baidu/PCL, 260B, Dec 2021, paper-only research checkpoint, never publicly served via API or HF).

Methodology this round (widened per instructions):
1. Read existing corpus record (verificationNotes) first — confirmed prior rounds and that this is a closed, apiAvailable:false, paper-only model.
2. Chinese-language sources: 腾讯新闻/Tencent News, CSDN blog, 百度百科/Baidu Baike, 知乎/Zhihu, 量子位/QbitAI, datalearner.com — all only repeat the paper's headline "outperforms SOTA on 68 NLP datasets" claim; none surface a standardized, taxonomy-mappable score.
3. Primary source: fetched and fully read the arXiv paper (2112.12731) as a PDF, all 20 pages, including Tables 2, 3, 4 (fine-tuning, few-shot FewCLUE, zero-shot results). These ARE real, citable, numeric results — but every one of the 68 datasets is a bespoke/component Chinese benchmark: AFQMC, CMNLI, OCNLI, CLUEWSC/CLUEWSC-FC, CHID/CHID-FC, DuReader (+robust/checklist/yesno), DRCD, CAIL2018-Task1/2, NLPCC2014-SC, SE-ABSA16, BDCI2019, COTE, FinRE, SanWen, LCQMC, BQ Corpus, IFLYTEK, TNEWS, THUCNEWS, CNSE, CNSS, CSLDCP, CSL/CSL-FC, BUSTM, EPRSTMT, cMedQA/cMedQA2, CKBQA, CKBQA-sub, WebQA, C3, CMRC2017/2018/2019, PD&CFT, WPLC, DogWhistle. None of these correspond to any taxonomy slug — in particular they predate and are not the same as the taxonomy's `c-eval` or `cmmlu` (both released 2023, after this Dec-2021 paper; the paper predates and never uses either).
4. Checked Artificial Analysis, LMArena/LMSYS, and OpenRouter directly — zero presence for this specific 260B checkpoint (expected, since it was never deployed for live inference/API access, unlike the newer ERNIE 4.x/5.x models that do appear on LMArena today).
5. Wayback Machine fetch was blocked in this environment (web.archive.org not fetchable), but no evidence anywhere suggests a dead standalone launch/leaderboard page ever existed for this specific paper-only checkpoint (it was announced via the arXiv paper and press coverage, not a product launch page).

Conclusion: this is a genuine "real data exists but doesn't fit the taxonomy" case, not a search-coverage gap. Recommend the corpus formally close this model out as "no trackable benchmark results" rather than continuing to re-search — the vendor simply never ran it against any standardized benchmark suite that maps to this taxonomy, and it was never served for third-party leaderboard evaluation. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): No new benchmark results found for ERNIE 3.0 Titan (Baidu/PCL, 260B, closed, paper-only, never publicly served) after checking the two specified structured sources plus two secondary sources:

1. Epoch AI raw data feed — fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (benchmark/task metadata only, 20 benchmarks defined, no per-model scores) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (the actual model-run results table, 2.3MB, 154 unique models covering benchmarks like FrontierMath, GPQA Diamond, MATH Level 5, SWE-bench Verified, AIME, etc.). Programmatically parsed the CSV's `Model` column (154 distinct model names) and full-text-searched the whole file case-insensitively for "ernie" and "baidu". Zero true matches: the only "ernie" hits (6) were substring false-positives inside the author name "Bernie Huang" in a Llama-3 author list; "baidu" had zero hits anywhere in the file. Confirme... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text] ROUND-14 HF-MODEL-CARD + SEMANTIC-SCHOLAR-CITATION CHECK (2026-07-16): Checked the two source types specified for this round; no new taxonomy-mappable results.

(1) HuggingFace model card: confirmed no HF repo exists for ERNIE 3.0 Titan itself. huggingface.co/models?search=titan+ernie and ?search=ernie-titan both return 0 results. The PaddlePaddle org page (163 models) contains only the six open-sourced ERNIE 3.0 distilled variants (XBase/Base/Medium/Mini/Micro/Nano, all Chinese-only small models, not Titan) plus OCR/doc-layout tooling - no 'titan' or '260B' entry. Third-party mirrors (nghuyong/*, swtx/ernie-3.0-base-chinese) are likewise only the small open-sourced ERNIE 3.0 checkpoints, not Titan. The only HF presence for 'ERNIE 3.0 Titan' is the auto-generated Paper page at huggingface.co/papers/2112.12731, which is a paper-abstract mirror (not a model card) and contains no eval-results metadata block - fetched it directly and it only reproduces the arXiv abstract plus a few Table 2/3/4 fine-tuning/few-shot/zero-shot example figures already captured in Round 7's PDF read (SE-ABSA16_PHNS 94.14%, CMRC2019 92.69/58.33 QAC/PAC, CAIL2018 Task1 93.18% F1-micro, TNEWS-FC 67.26%, CLUEWSC-FC 90.57%, CMRC2018 30.41 avg EM/F1, CKBQA 24.12%) - none map to taxonomy slugs (bespoke Chinese benchmarks, not c-eval/cmmlu which postdate this Dec-2021 paper). No eval_results.json or Files-and-versions eval artifact exists since there is no repo to hold one.

(2) Semantic Scholar citation search: queried api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/arXiv:2112.12731/citations (100 citing papers returned, 2021-2026). Scanned for NLP papers likely to carry a head-to-head comparison table. Most promising hit: GLM-130B (arXiv 2210.02414, Zeng et al.) - its abstract explicitly states 'It also consistently and significantly outperforms ERNIE TITAN 3.0 260B -- the largest Chinese language model -- across related benchmarks.' Fetched the full paper (PDF via arxiv.org/pdf and HTML via ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2210.02414) and located Section 5.4 / Table 8 / Figure 8: GLM-130B reports only AGGREGATE relative deltas versus ERNIE Titan 3.0 - '+24.26%' average over 7 zero-shot CLUE datasets and '+12.75%' average over 5 zero-shot FewCLUE datasets (plus a specific claim of '260% better' on DRCD/CMRC2018 abstractive MRC) - not ERNIE Titan's own absolute per-task scores broken out numerically in extractable table form, and even if they were, CLUE/FewCLUE are not taxonomy slugs (taxonomy has c-eval/cmmlu/superclue, all benchmarks that postdate this Dec-2021 model and that Titan was never evaluated on, since it was never served for third-party inference). Checked three other citing papers with full-text fetch (M3KE arXiv 2305.10263, PanGu-Sigma arXiv 2303.10845, WeLM arXiv 2209.10372) - none confirmed to mention ERNIE 3.0 Titan by name in accessible excerpts (M3KE evaluates only 335M-130B open-source Chinese LLMs; PanGu-Sigma and WeLM abstracts don't reference Titan). CMMLU paper (arXiv 2306.09212) fetched directly and confirmed to NOT mention ERNIE 3.0 Titan anywhere.

Conclusion: both specified source types checked exhaustively; no citable, taxonomy-mappable benchmark result exists for ERNIE 3.0 Titan. This model has no HF repo (never open-sourced/served) and the only third-party academic comparison found (GLM-130B) reports aggregate deltas on non-taxonomy Chinese benchmarks (CLUE/FewCLUE), not absolute scores on a taxonomy slug. Recommend no further rounds targeting HF-repo or citation-search methodologies for this specific closed, paper-only 260B checkpoint.

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] No new benchmark results found for ERNIE 3.0 Titan (Baidu/PCL, 260B, closed, paper-only, arXiv 2112.12731). Both specified source types were checked directly and exhaustively:

(1) HuggingFace model card: This model has NO HuggingFace repo. Confirmed via huggingface.co/models?search=titan+ernie and ?search=ernie-titan, both returning 0 results. The PaddlePaddle org (163 models, the official ERNIE publisher on HF) only hosts the six small open-sourced ERNIE 3.0 distilled variants (XBase/Base/Medium/Mini/Micro/Nano) plus unrelated OCR/doc-layout models -- Titan itself was never open-sourced or served, so no repo, README, or eval_results.json exists for it. The only HF trace is the auto-generated Paper page at huggingface.co/papers/2112.12731 (a paper-abstract mirror, not a model card, no eval-metadata table). Fetched it directly -- it reproduces sample fine-tuning/few-shot/zero-shot numbers already found and logged in a prior round's direct PDF read (e.g. SE-ABSA16_PHNS 94.14% accuracy, CAIL2018 Task1 93.18% F1-micro, CKBQA 24.12% accuracy) -- these are all bespoke Chinese NLU datasets that don't correspond to any taxonomy slug and predate c-eval/cmmlu (both 2023, after this Dec-2021 paper).

(2) Semantic Scholar citation search: Queried the citations endpoint for arXiv:2112.12731 and got 100 citing papers (2021-2026). The one substantive hit was GLM-130B (arXiv 2210.02414, Zeng et al.), whose abstract states verbatim: "It also consistently and significantly outperforms ERNIE TITAN 3.0 260B -- the largest Chinese language model -- across related benchmarks." Fetched the full paper and located the relevant section (5.4 / Table 8 / Figure 8): GLM-130B reports only AGGREGATE relative deltas versus ERNIE Titan 3.0 -- "+24.26%" average over 7 zero-shot CLUE datasets and "+12.75%" average over 5 zero-shot FewCLUE datasets -- not ERNIE Titan's own absolute per-task numeric scores in extractable table form. Even if extracted, CLUE/FewCLUE are not taxonomy slugs. Also checked three other promising citing papers by full-text fetch (M3KE arXiv 2305.10263, PanGu-Sigma arXiv 2303.10845, WeLM arXiv 2209.10372) and the CMMLU paper directly (arXiv 2306.09212) -- none contain an ERNIE 3.0 Titan comparison with taxonomy-mappable scores; CMMLU confirmed to not mention it at all.

Conclusion: this remains a genuine "real published numbers exist but none map to the taxonomy, and no HF/API access ever existed for independent re-evaluation" case, not a search-coverage gap. Full search trail appended to corpus/models/baidu/ernie-3-0-titan.json verificationNotes (Round 14, 2026-07-16). No further HF-repo or citation-search rounds recommended for this specific closed, paper-only checkpoint.
